case_topic: Broadband ISP consistently delivering fraction of contracted speed — refund and compensation
case_description: My name is Rohit Agarwal, resident of Whitefield, Bengaluru. I subscribed to Velocity Telecom's VelocityFiber 200 Mbps Unlimited plan (Account No. VTL/BLR/BB/2022/004812) at Rs. 999/month + GST, paying Rs. 12,000 annual advance in January 2023 and again in January 2024. Throughout 2023 the actual download speed consistently ranged between 6-20 Mbps against the contracted 200 Mbps — documented by Ookla Speedtest screenshots. I filed 5 complaints with the ISP (complaint nos. on record) and also escalated to TRAI portal (Ref: TRAI/DG/2023/00445) in October 2023 with no resolution. The ISP attributed the issue to 'network congestion' without rectifying it. In January 2024 the subscription was auto-renewed without my explicit consent, charging another Rs. 12,000. My legal notice dated 15-Feb-2024 demanded: (1) pro-rata refund for 9 months of substandard service Rs. 9,000, (2) refund of 2024 advance Rs. 12,000 (no consent for renewal), (3) Rs. 15,000 compensation for work-from-home productivity loss — total Rs. 36,000. The ISP did not respond within 30 days. I am a work-from-home software professional — the service deficiency directly impacted my income.
